behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit beats Integration because this can be asserted from one generated artifact; no module handoff is required to expose the core failure. Agentic beats System because the failure boundary is model judgment (whether the LLM follows interface constraints), not OS/network/subprocess behavior. Rejected Workflow: behavior requires one model output check, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run live generation for fixed specs, parse the produced signature, and assert exact match on parameter names/types and return type across repeated runs to catch model-output failures.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because the failure appears at module boundaries (orchestrator, test-result consumer, attempt bookkeeping). Integration beats System because real OS/network/subprocess is not required; deterministic fakes can reproduce ordering/early-exit bugs. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: correctness is orchestration control flow, not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use stub generator and stub test executor to force fail/pass sequence; assert attempt order, stop-on-first-pass behavior, and no extra calls after success.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because multi-module loop state and retries are essential. Agentic beats System because success depends on real model output quality, not infrastructure. Workflow beats Agentic because multiple real model decisions are sequenced (generate -> fail -> refine -> retry) and handoff correctness is part of the failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real loop against a known task where first attempt commonly fails; require eventual pass within <=3 attempts and verify per-attempt state transitions.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because the real risk is handoff from test results into prompt-construction inputs, not only string formatting logic. Integration beats System because no real OS/network/subprocess is required to detect missing failure context. Rejected Agentic: behavior text is about prompt content inclusion, not whether the model makes correct decisions from that prompt.
behaviors.test_description: Inject failing test results through the normal orchestration path and assert the exact failed test names/messages are present in the next prompt payload sent to the model client.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because prior-attempt context must flow across modules into the next attempt. Agentic beats System because the key failure is model behavioral response to context. Workflow beats Agentic because this explicitly requires at least two linked model steps (initial attempt + context-aware retry). Rejected Integration: mocked model could always return changed code and hide real non-responsiveness.
behaviors.test_description: Run live attempt 1 to failure, feed real failure context into attempt 2, and assert attempt-2 code differs materially and addresses cited failure signals (not a byte-identical resubmission).

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because this validates orchestration, attempt persistence, and error assembly across components. Integration beats System because deterministic in-process stubs can trigger three failures and verify terminal response shape; no real OS/network dependency is required. Rejected Agentic: model quality is irrelevant if failures are forced via stubs.
behaviors.test_description: Force three consecutive failed attempts with deterministic stubs; assert termination at attempt 3 and validate structured error contains all attempt artifacts and test outcomes.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because this is explicitly full-pipeline wiring, not isolated logic. System beats Integration because end-to-end test execution typically crosses real subprocess/filesystem boundaries, and those failures are often hidden by in-process fakes. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior explicitly says non-agentic with deterministic model stubs.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end with stubbed model outputs but real code execution path (temp files/test runner invocation); assert generated code is executed, results are captured, and attempt bookkeeping is consistent.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because this is full-loop orchestration across modules. Agentic beats System because correctness depends on real model improvement quality, not just infrastructure. Workflow beats Agentic because multiple model decisions are chained (initial generation and refinement cycle), and success depends on that sequence. Rejected lower levels: deterministic stubs cannot validate real iterative improvement.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full live pipeline on a curated hard task with known first-attempt failure; require eventual pass after one or more refinements within the attempt budget.
